api-dev.title: Internet & How the Web Works
api-dev.description: Before writing a single line of backend code, understand the infrastructure you're building on. Learn how data travels across the internet, what happens when you hit an API endpoint, and the protocols that power the web.
topics: How the internet works — packets, routers, ISPs
DNS — domain resolution, A records, TTL
TCP vs UDP — connections, handshakes, reliability
REST architecture — resources, statelessness, uniform interface
Browsers vs API clients — curl, Postman, Insomnia
api-dev.challenge: Use curl to make GET, POST, and DELETE requests against a public API (e.g. JSONPlaceholder). Inspect headers and status codes for each.

api-dev.title: OS, Terminal & Linux Basics
api-dev.description: APIs run on servers. You need to be comfortable with the command line, file system navigation, process management, and basic Linux administration to work effectively on any backend.
topics: File system navigation — ls, cd, mkdir, rm, chmod
Text processing — grep, awk, sed, cat, pipe
Process management — ps, kill, top, background jobs
SSH — key generation, remote login, scp
Shell scripting basics — variables, loops, conditionals

resources.label: Missing Semester of CS Education — MIT

api-dev.challenge: Write a bash script that reads a log file, extracts all lines with ERROR, counts them, and outputs a summary with a timestamp.

api-dev.title: Node.js Fundamentals
api-dev.description: Node.js is the runtime your API will live in. Learn how it works under the hood — the event loop, async I/O, module systems — and build confidence writing server-side JavaScript.
topics: Node.js architecture — event loop, libuv, non-blocking I/O
ESM vs CommonJS — import/require, module resolution
Built-in modules — fs, path, http, events, stream
Error handling patterns — try/catch, Promise rejection
Debugging Node.js — inspector, VS Code debugger
api-dev.challenge: Build a raw HTTP server (no Express) that serves a JSON API with two endpoints — one that reads a file asynchronously and one that returns server stats.

api-dev.title: Express & REST API Design
api-dev.description: Build well-structured, maintainable REST APIs with Express. Learn routing patterns, middleware chains, request validation, and how to structure a production-grade API codebase.
topics: Express routing — router.get/post/put/delete, params, query strings
Middleware — logging (morgan), body parsing, error handling
Controller pattern — separating route logic from business logic
Input validation with Zod or Joi
REST conventions — resource naming, status codes, HATEOAS basics
api-dev.challenge: Build a Books REST API — CRUD endpoints, input validation with Zod, a custom error-handling middleware, and a request logger.

api-dev.title: Databases — SQL & NoSQL
api-dev.description: Choose the right database for the job. Understand relational vs document models, write efficient queries, design schemas, and connect databases to your Node.js API.
topics: Relational model — tables, primary/foreign keys, normalization
SQL — SELECT, JOIN, GROUP BY, transactions, EXPLAIN
MongoDB with the native driver or Mongoose
Indexing strategies — B-tree, compound, partial indexes
Database design patterns — 1-N, N-M, embedding vs referencing
api-dev.challenge: Add PostgreSQL to your Books API — design a normalized schema with authors and books, write raw SQL queries, and add indexes for frequently-filtered columns.

api-dev.title: Authentication & Security
api-dev.description: Secure your API against real-world threats. Implement auth flows, hash passwords properly, understand OAuth, and apply OWASP principles to harden your endpoints.
topics: Password hashing — bcrypt, argon2, why salts matter
JWT — structure, signing algorithms (HS256 / RS256), expiry
OAuth 2.0 flows — authorization code, client credentials
OWASP API Top 10 — injection, broken auth, rate limiting

resources.label: Web Dev Simplified — JWT Auth Tutorial

api-dev.challenge: Add full auth to your Books API — register/login with hashed passwords, JWT access + refresh tokens, protected routes, and rate limiting on the login endpoint.

api-dev.title: Caching & Performance
api-dev.description: Make your API fast and resilient. Learn caching at the application and HTTP layers, use Redis for session storage and rate limiting, and optimize response times.
topics: Redis data structures — strings, hashes, sorted sets, TTL
HTTP cache headers — Cache-Control, ETag, Last-Modified
Rate limiting strategies — fixed window, sliding window, token bucket
api-dev.challenge: Add Redis caching to your Books API — cache expensive queries with a 5-minute TTL, invalidate on mutations, and implement rate limiting per IP.

api-dev.title: Testing & API Documentation
api-dev.description: Ensure your API works correctly and is understandable to consumers. Write integration tests, automate test runs, and generate interactive documentation with OpenAPI/Swagger.
topics: Unit testing with Jest — describe, it, expect
Integration testing with Supertest — test real HTTP endpoints
Test database setup — in-memory SQLite or test MongoDB
Mocking external services with Jest spies
api-dev.challenge: Write full integration tests for your Books API — cover all CRUD endpoints, auth flows, and edge cases. Add Swagger UI with documented schemas.

api-dev.title: Message Queues & Background Jobs
api-dev.description: Not every operation needs to be synchronous. Learn how to offload heavy tasks to background workers, use message queues, and implement webhooks for event-driven integrations.
topics: Webhooks — sending & receiving with signature verification
api-dev.challenge: Add an email welcome queue to your Books API — when a user registers, enqueue a job that sends a welcome email with retry logic and a dead-letter fallback.

api-dev.title: Deployment & Production Readiness
api-dev.description: Ship your API to production with confidence. Containerize with Docker, automate deployments with CI/CD, and set up logging and monitoring so you can observe your running service.
topics: Docker Compose — local multi-service dev (app + db + redis)
Railway / Render / Fly.io — zero-config cloud deployment
GitHub Actions CI — lint, test, build on every push
Structured logging with Pino or Winston
APM basics — error tracking with Sentry

resources.label: TechWorld with Nana — Docker for Beginners

api-dev.challenge: Dockerize your Books API with a multi-stage Dockerfile, write a docker-compose.yml for local dev (app + postgres + redis), and deploy to Railway with a GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

db-engineer.title: Database Fundamentals
db-engineer.description: Start with what a database is and why it matters. Learn the core concepts shared by every relational system before diving into a specific engine.
db-engineer.challenge: Design a normalized schema for a small e-commerce store on paper, then create the tables.

db-engineer.title: SQL Mastery
db-engineer.description: Become fluent in SQL — the language you'll use every day. Move from basic selects to complex analytical queries.
db-engineer.challenge: Answer 10 analytical business questions about your store using window functions and CTEs.

db-engineer.title: Relational Modeling & Normalization
db-engineer.description: Design schemas that scale and stay consistent. Master normalization, relationships and constraints.
db-engineer.challenge: Normalize a messy spreadsheet of orders into a clean relational schema with proper constraints.

db-engineer.title: PostgreSQL Deep Dive
db-engineer.description: Master the world's most advanced open-source database. Explore the features that make PostgreSQL a power tool.
db-engineer.challenge: Build a search feature over your store's products using full-text search and JSONB attributes.

db-engineer.title: MySQL & Operational Databases
db-engineer.description: Learn MySQL, the backbone of countless production apps. Understand its storage engines and operational behavior.
db-engineer.challenge: Migrate a schema to MySQL and demonstrate transaction isolation behavior with concurrent sessions.

db-engineer.title: NoSQL with MongoDB
db-engineer.description: Add document databases to your toolkit. Learn when NoSQL fits and how to model data in MongoDB.
db-engineer.challenge: Model a content platform in MongoDB and write an aggregation pipeline for a trending feed.

db-engineer.title: Caching & Redis
db-engineer.description: Speed up systems with in-memory data. Use Redis for caching, sessions, queues and real-time features.
db-engineer.challenge: Add a Redis cache-aside layer and a leaderboard to your app, with sensible TTLs and invalidation.

db-engineer.title: Indexing & Query Optimization
db-engineer.description: Make databases fast. Understand indexes deeply and learn to diagnose and fix slow queries.
db-engineer.challenge: Profile a slow query, add the right index, and cut its execution time using EXPLAIN ANALYZE.

db-engineer.title: Replication, Sharding & HA
db-engineer.description: Scale databases beyond one machine. Learn replication, partitioning and high-availability patterns.
db-engineer.challenge: Set up streaming replication with a read replica and route read queries to it.

db-engineer.title: Backup, Security & Administration
db-engineer.description: Operate databases responsibly. Master backups, security hardening and monitoring for production.
db-engineer.challenge: Build a backup + restore runbook and apply least-privilege roles to your production schema.

systems-dev.title: Systems Programming Foundations
systems-dev.description: Systems programming starts close to the metal. Understand how programs are built, how memory is laid out, and how code becomes machine instructions.

resources.label: Ben Eater — How a CPU works

systems-dev.challenge: Write a C program that manipulates raw bytes and explain its memory layout with a diagram.

systems-dev.title: C & the Memory Model
systems-dev.description: C is the lingua franca of systems. Master manual memory management, pointers and the tools that keep them safe.
systems-dev.challenge: Implement a dynamic array and a linked list in C with zero memory leaks (verified by valgrind).

systems-dev.title: Modern C++
systems-dev.description: Build on C with modern C++. Use RAII, smart pointers and the STL to write fast, safer systems code.
systems-dev.challenge: Rewrite your C data structures in modern C++ using RAII and smart pointers.

systems-dev.title: Rust for Systems
systems-dev.description: Get memory safety without a garbage collector. Learn how Rust's ownership model prevents whole classes of bugs.
systems-dev.challenge: Port one of your C++ data structures to Rust and call it from C via FFI.

systems-dev.title: Linux & the OS Interface
systems-dev.description: Systems software talks to the kernel. Learn the Linux process model, system calls and the file system interface.
systems-dev.challenge: Write a mini-shell in C that forks processes, handles pipes and reaps children.

systems-dev.title: Concurrency & Parallelism
systems-dev.description: Make systems use all the cores. Master threads, synchronization and lock-free techniques.
topics: Data races & how to avoid them
systems-dev.challenge: Build a thread-safe work-stealing task queue and benchmark it against a mutex-guarded queue.

systems-dev.title: Networking & Sockets
systems-dev.description: Connect systems over the network. Learn the socket API and how to build high-throughput servers.
systems-dev.challenge: Build a non-blocking TCP echo server with epoll that handles thousands of connections.

systems-dev.title: Performance & Profiling
systems-dev.description: Make code fast and prove it. Understand the memory hierarchy and use profilers to find real bottlenecks.
systems-dev.challenge: Profile a hot loop, improve data locality, and document the speedup with flamegraphs.

systems-dev.title: Building Systems Tools in Go
systems-dev.description: Go is ideal for fast, portable systems tooling. Build CLIs and network tools that ship as a single binary.
systems-dev.challenge: Build a concurrent port scanner CLI in Go that cross-compiles to Linux, macOS and Windows.

systems-dev.title: Debugging, Testing & Deployment
systems-dev.description: Ship reliable systems software. Master debuggers, sanitizers and build pipelines for native code.
systems-dev.challenge: Add a CI pipeline that builds your project with sanitizers and runs a fuzz target.

security-dev.title: Security Foundations
security-dev.description: Build a mental model of security. Learn the core principles and the threat landscape every developer must understand.
security-dev.challenge: Threat-model a simple login form and list its assets, threats and mitigations.

security-dev.title: Web Security & OWASP Top 10
security-dev.description: Learn the most common web vulnerabilities by exploiting and then fixing them. The OWASP Top 10 is your map.
security-dev.challenge: Find and fix an XSS and a SQL injection vulnerability in OWASP Juice Shop.

security-dev.title: Authentication
security-dev.description: Get authentication right. Implement secure login flows with OAuth2, OpenID Connect and JSON Web Tokens.
security-dev.challenge: Implement an OAuth2 + OIDC login with secure JWT handling and refresh-token rotation.

security-dev.title: Authorization & Access Control
security-dev.description: Control who can do what. Design robust authorization models and avoid the #1 web risk: broken access control.
security-dev.challenge: Add object-level RBAC to an API and write tests proving users can't access others' resources.

security-dev.title: Cryptography Essentials
security-dev.description: Use cryptography correctly. Learn the primitives, when to use each, and the dangers of rolling your own.
security-dev.challenge: Encrypt sensitive fields at rest with AES-GCM and verify integrity with HMAC.

security-dev.title: Secure Coding Practices
security-dev.description: Write code that resists attack. Apply input validation, output encoding and safe defaults throughout your app.
security-dev.challenge: Harden a vulnerable API endpoint with validation, parameterized queries and proper secret handling.

security-dev.title: API & Network Security
security-dev.description: Protect data in transit and APIs at the edge. Configure TLS, CORS and defenses against abuse.
security-dev.challenge: Configure TLS, strict CORS, a CSP and rate limiting on an API, then verify with a header scanner.

security-dev.title: Threat Modeling & Pen Testing
security-dev.description: Think like an attacker. Model threats systematically and learn the basics of ethical penetration testing.
security-dev.challenge: Produce a STRIDE threat model and a short pen-test report for a demo web app.

security-dev.title: Security Testing & DevSecOps
security-dev.description: Automate security in the pipeline. Add static, dynamic and dependency scanning so issues are caught early.
security-dev.challenge: Add SAST, dependency scanning and secret scanning as blocking checks to a CI pipeline.

security-dev.title: Incident Response & Compliance
security-dev.description: Prepare for when things go wrong. Build logging, an incident response plan, and awareness of compliance regimes.
security-dev.challenge: Add security logging to an app and write an incident response runbook for a credential leak.

graphql-dev.title: Backend & API Foundations
graphql-dev.description: Before GraphQL, you need a solid backend base. Understand HTTP, build a basic Node service, and know what REST does well so you can see what GraphQL improves.
graphql-dev.challenge: Build a tiny REST API, then list every place it over-fetches or needs multiple round trips.

graphql-dev.title: GraphQL Fundamentals
graphql-dev.description: Learn the GraphQL query language and type system from the client's perspective before building a server.
graphql-dev.challenge: Use a public GraphQL API to write queries with variables, fragments and nested fields.

graphql-dev.title: Schema Design with SDL
graphql-dev.description: A GraphQL API is only as good as its schema. Design expressive, evolvable schemas with the Schema Definition Language.
graphql-dev.challenge: Design the schema for a blog with users, posts, comments and cursor-based pagination.

graphql-dev.title: Resolvers & Context
graphql-dev.description: Resolvers are where a schema meets your data. Learn the resolver chain, context, and how arguments flow through.
topics: Resolver signature — parent, args, context, info
graphql-dev.challenge: Implement resolvers for your blog schema against in-memory data, including nested post→author resolution.

graphql-dev.title: Apollo Server
graphql-dev.description: Stand up a real GraphQL server with Apollo Server, wire it to Express, and add typed schema and resolvers.
graphql-dev.challenge: Serve your blog schema with Apollo Server + TypeScript and generate types with GraphQL Code Generator.

graphql-dev.title: Data Layer with Prisma & DataLoader
graphql-dev.description: Back your API with a real database and solve the infamous N+1 problem with batching and caching.
graphql-dev.challenge: Move your blog to PostgreSQL with Prisma and eliminate N+1 queries on post→author with DataLoader.

graphql-dev.title: Mutations, Subscriptions & Real-time
graphql-dev.description: Make your API write data and push live updates. Implement mutations and real-time subscriptions over WebSockets.
graphql-dev.challenge: Add create/update/delete mutations and a live 'new comment' subscription to your blog API.

graphql-dev.title: Authentication & Authorization
graphql-dev.description: Secure a GraphQL API where a single endpoint exposes everything. Authenticate users and authorize at the field level.
graphql-dev.challenge: Add JWT auth to your API and restrict post editing to authors with field-level authorization rules.

graphql-dev.title: Performance, Caching & Security
graphql-dev.description: Production GraphQL needs guards. Add caching, limit expensive queries, and defend against malicious operations.
graphql-dev.challenge: Add query complexity limits, response caching and persisted queries, then load-test the result.

graphql-dev.title: Testing, Federation & Deployment
graphql-dev.description: Ship and scale your GraphQL API. Test operations, split a monolith with federation, and deploy.
graphql-dev.challenge: Add an integration test suite, split one type into a federated subgraph, and deploy the gateway.

microservices.title: Microservices Foundations
microservices.description: Understand what microservices are and when they help. Compare them with monoliths and learn the trade-offs before building.
topics: Benefits, costs & fallacies of distributed systems
microservices.challenge: Take a sample monolith and map out how you'd decompose it into 3–4 bounded-context services.

microservices.title: Service Design & DDD
microservices.description: Design services that own their domain. Apply domain-driven design to find boundaries and define clean contracts.
microservices.challenge: Define the API contract and data ownership for two of your decomposed services.

microservices.title: Inter-Service Communication
microservices.description: Connect services reliably. Choose between REST and gRPC and design robust synchronous communication.
microservices.challenge: Implement a gRPC contract between two services with timeouts, retries and a contract test.

microservices.title: Messaging & Event-Driven
microservices.description: Decouple services with asynchronous messaging. Use RabbitMQ or Kafka to build event-driven architectures.
microservices.challenge: Wire your services together with an event bus so one publishes events the other consumes.

microservices.title: Containerization with Docker
microservices.description: Package each service as a container. Build images, run multi-service stacks locally, and standardize environments.
microservices.challenge: Containerize all your services and run the whole system locally with Docker Compose.

microservices.title: Orchestration with Kubernetes
microservices.description: Run services at scale. Deploy, scale and self-heal your containers with Kubernetes.
microservices.challenge: Deploy your service stack to Kubernetes with deployments, services, config and a Helm chart.

microservices.title: API Gateway & Service Mesh
microservices.description: Manage cross-cutting concerns at the edge and between services with gateways and a service mesh.
topics: Routing, auth & rate limiting at the edge
microservices.challenge: Put an API gateway in front of your services with centralized auth and rate limiting.

microservices.title: Data Management & Saga
microservices.description: Handle data consistency across services. Implement distributed transactions with the saga pattern.
microservices.challenge: Implement an order/payment saga across two services with compensating transactions.

microservices.title: Observability & Resilience
microservices.description: Operate distributed systems with confidence. Add metrics, logs and traces, and build in resilience.
microservices.challenge: Instrument every service with tracing and metrics, then add circuit breakers to remote calls.

microservices.title: CI/CD & Deployment
microservices.description: Automate delivery of many services. Build pipelines, adopt GitOps, and deploy safely and often.
microservices.challenge: Build CI/CD for each service and deploy the whole system to Kubernetes via GitOps with ArgoCD.

go-dev.title: Go Fundamentals
go-dev.description: Start with Go's clean, minimal syntax. Learn the type system, control flow, and how Go's simplicity is its superpower.
go-dev.challenge: Build a CLI tool that reads a text file and prints word, line and character counts.

go-dev.title: Tooling & Project Structure
go-dev.description: Learn the toolchain that makes Go productive — modules, formatting, linting and the standard project layout.
go-dev.challenge: Restructure your CLI tool into a proper module with packages, formatted code, and a passing go vet.

go-dev.title: Structs, Interfaces & Generics
go-dev.description: Model your domain the Go way. Compose behavior with interfaces and write reusable code with generics.
go-dev.challenge: Build a generic in-memory store with a Repository interface and two concrete implementations.

go-dev.title: Concurrency — Goroutines & Channels
go-dev.description: Concurrency is Go's headline feature. Master goroutines, channels and the patterns for safe concurrent code.
go-dev.challenge: Build a concurrent URL checker that fetches a list of sites in parallel with a worker pool and a timeout.

go-dev.title: Building HTTP Services
go-dev.description: Build web services with Go's standard library, then add a router like Gin or Chi for ergonomics.
go-dev.challenge: Build a JSON REST API for a bookmarks service with routing, middleware and graceful shutdown.

go-dev.title: Working with Databases
go-dev.description: Persist data from Go. Use database/sql and pgx for PostgreSQL, and generate type-safe queries with sqlc.
go-dev.challenge: Back your bookmarks API with PostgreSQL using sqlc-generated queries and migration files.

go-dev.title: API Design, Middleware & Auth
go-dev.description: Turn a working service into a robust API with consistent design, authentication and request validation.
go-dev.challenge: Add JWT auth, input validation and rate limiting to the bookmarks API with consistent JSON errors.

go-dev.title: Testing & Benchmarking
go-dev.description: Write tests the idiomatic Go way — table-driven tests, mocks, benchmarks and coverage.
topics: Integration tests with a real database
go-dev.challenge: Add table-driven unit tests, httptest handler tests, and a benchmark to your API, running with -race.

go-dev.title: Observability & Performance
go-dev.description: Make your service observable and fast. Add structured logging, metrics and tracing, and profile hot paths.
go-dev.challenge: Instrument the bookmarks API with slog, expose Prometheus metrics, and profile one endpoint with pprof.

go-dev.title: Containers, gRPC & Deployment
go-dev.description: Ship your Go service. Build tiny container images, expose a gRPC interface, and automate deployment.
topics: Multi-stage Docker builds for tiny images
go-dev.challenge: Containerize the API with a multi-stage build, add a gRPC endpoint, and deploy via a GitHub Actions pipeline.

rust-dev.title: Rust Fundamentals
rust-dev.description: Begin with Rust's syntax and the toolchain. Learn variables, types and control flow while getting comfortable with the compiler.
topics: Control flow — if, loop, while, for
rust-dev.challenge: Build a CLI temperature converter that parses arguments and handles invalid input gracefully.

rust-dev.title: Ownership, Borrowing & Lifetimes
rust-dev.description: The heart of Rust. Understand ownership, references and lifetimes — the rules that give Rust memory safety without a garbage collector.
rust-dev.challenge: Write a function library that manipulates strings and vectors using only borrows — no unnecessary clones.

rust-dev.title: Traits, Generics & Error Handling
rust-dev.description: Write reusable, expressive Rust. Abstract behavior with traits and generics, and handle failure with Result and the ? operator.
rust-dev.challenge: Build a config parser that returns a custom error enum and propagates failures with the ? operator.

rust-dev.title: Collections, Iterators & Closures
rust-dev.description: Use Rust's powerful standard library. Chain iterators, capture environment with closures, and pick the right collection.
rust-dev.challenge: Build a word-frequency counter that reads a file and prints the top 10 words using iterator chains.

rust-dev.title: Cargo, Crates & Modules
rust-dev.description: Organize and share real Rust projects. Master the module system, workspaces and the crate ecosystem.
rust-dev.challenge: Split your word-counter into a library + binary crate, add serde and clap, and document the public API.

rust-dev.title: Async Rust with Tokio
rust-dev.description: Write high-performance async Rust. Understand futures, the async/await model, and the Tokio runtime.
rust-dev.challenge: Build an async TCP echo server with Tokio that handles many concurrent connections.

rust-dev.title: Web Services with Axum & Actix
rust-dev.description: Build production web services in Rust. Create routes, extractors and middleware with Axum or Actix Web.
rust-dev.challenge: Build a JSON REST API for a link shortener with Axum, including extractors and typed error responses.

rust-dev.title: Databases with SQLx & Diesel
rust-dev.description: Persist data safely. Use SQLx for compile-time-checked queries or Diesel's ORM against PostgreSQL.
rust-dev.challenge: Back your link shortener with PostgreSQL using SQLx, with migrations and a connection pool.

rust-dev.title: Testing, Benchmarking & Unsafe
rust-dev.description: Ensure correctness and speed. Write tests and benchmarks, and learn when (and when not) to reach for unsafe.
rust-dev.challenge: Add an integration test suite and a criterion benchmark to your API, then profile the hot path.

rust-dev.title: WebAssembly & Production Deployment
rust-dev.description: Take Rust to the browser and to production. Compile to WebAssembly and deploy a lean, fast Rust service.
rust-dev.challenge: Compile a Rust module to WebAssembly and call it from a web page, then containerize your API for deployment.

java-spring.title: Java Fundamentals
java-spring.description: Start with the Java language and the JVM. Learn syntax, types and the compile-run cycle that underpins Spring.
java-spring.challenge: Build a CLI bank account simulator with deposits, withdrawals and validation.

java-spring.title: OOP & Core Java
java-spring.description: Model your domain with object-oriented Java. Master classes, inheritance, interfaces and exception handling.
java-spring.challenge: Refactor your bank simulator into a clean OOP model with interfaces and custom exceptions.

java-spring.title: Collections, Generics & Streams
java-spring.description: Use Java's powerful standard library. Work with collections, write generic code, and process data with the Stream API.
topics: The Stream API — map, filter, collect
java-spring.challenge: Process a list of transactions with streams to produce per-category totals and summaries.

java-spring.title: Build Tools & Spring Basics
java-spring.description: Enter the Spring ecosystem. Manage builds with Maven and understand dependency injection — Spring's core idea.
java-spring.challenge: Create a Spring Boot project with Maven and wire two beans together via constructor injection.

java-spring.title: Spring Boot & REST APIs
java-spring.description: Build production REST APIs with Spring Boot. Create controllers, handle requests, and validate input.
java-spring.challenge: Build a REST API for a library service with controllers, DTOs, validation and global error handling.

java-spring.title: Spring Data & JPA/Hibernate
java-spring.description: Persist data the Spring way. Map entities with JPA/Hibernate and query effortlessly with Spring Data repositories.
java-spring.challenge: Back your library API with PostgreSQL using JPA entities, repositories and Flyway migrations.

java-spring.title: Spring Security
java-spring.description: Secure your Spring application. Configure authentication and authorization with Spring Security and JWT.
java-spring.challenge: Add JWT authentication and role-based authorization to your library API.

java-spring.title: Testing
java-spring.description: Test Spring applications thoroughly with JUnit, Mockito and Testcontainers for realistic integration tests.
java-spring.challenge: Write unit tests with Mockito and an integration test with Testcontainers against real PostgreSQL.

java-spring.title: Messaging & Microservices
java-spring.description: Scale Spring into distributed systems. Communicate via messaging and adopt microservice patterns with Spring Cloud.
java-spring.challenge: Split one feature into a second service communicating over Kafka with circuit-breaker protection.

java-spring.title: Packaging & Deployment
java-spring.description: Ship your Spring app. Build container images, expose health and metrics, and automate deployment.
java-spring.challenge: Containerize your service with a layered image, expose Actuator metrics, and deploy via CI.

python-backend.title: Python Fundamentals
python-backend.description: Python is the most readable language in backend development. Master its syntax, type system, built-in data structures, and the packaging ecosystem before diving into frameworks.
topics: Lists, tuples, sets, dicts — operations and comprehensions
Functions — args, kwargs, default values, return
File I/O — open, read, write, with statement

resources.label: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

python-backend.challenge: Build a CLI task manager in Python — add, list, mark complete, and delete tasks, persisted to a JSON file.

python-backend.title: Python Advanced
python-backend.description: Go deeper into Python's powerful features. Writing idiomatic Python — with clean OOP, decorators, generators, and type hints — is what separates junior from senior Python developers.
topics: OOP — classes, inheritance, dunder methods, dataclasses
Decorators — function decorators, wraps, parameterized decorators
Type hints — annotations, Optional, Union, TypedDict, mypy
Error handling — custom exceptions, exception chaining
python-backend.challenge: Refactor your CLI task manager using a proper class-based architecture with decorators for logging and a custom TaskNotFound exception.

python-backend.title: FastAPI & API Design
python-backend.description: FastAPI is the modern Python web framework — it's fast, typed, async-first, and auto-generates OpenAPI documentation. Learn to build clean, validated REST APIs.
topics: FastAPI project structure — routers, dependencies, lifespan
Pydantic models — BaseModel, validators, field types
Path parameters, query params, request body
python-backend.challenge: Build a Products REST API with FastAPI — CRUD endpoints, Pydantic schemas for validation, and structured error responses.

python-backend.title: Databases & SQLAlchemy
python-backend.description: Persist your data reliably. Learn PostgreSQL, the most powerful open-source relational database, and SQLAlchemy's async ORM to model your data with migrations.
topics: PostgreSQL basics — schemas, tables, constraints, indexes
SQLAlchemy Core vs ORM — declarative models
Alembic migrations — creating, applying, rolling back
Query optimization — EXPLAIN ANALYZE, N+1 avoidance
python-backend.challenge: Connect your Products API to PostgreSQL using async SQLAlchemy — define ORM models, write Alembic migrations, and add relationships to a categories table.

python-backend.title: Authentication & Security
python-backend.description: Secure your FastAPI backend. Implement OAuth2 password flow, hash passwords with bcrypt, issue JWTs, and protect routes with dependency-based authentication.
python-backend.challenge: Add auth to your Products API — register/login with hashed passwords, JWT tokens, an /me endpoint, and role-based access (admin can delete products).

python-backend.title: Async Python & Celery
python-backend.description: Python's async capabilities are powerful for I/O-heavy backends. Learn asyncio deeply, then use Celery and Redis to offload background processing.
topics: asyncio — event loop, coroutines, async/await, gather
Celery tasks — delay, apply_async, retry, ETA
python-backend.challenge: Add a Celery task that sends a confirmation email after product creation, with Redis as the broker and Flower for task monitoring.

python-backend.title: Testing with pytest
python-backend.description: pytest is the gold standard for Python testing. Write clean, readable tests for your FastAPI endpoints, database operations, and background tasks.
topics: Test database — SQLite in-memory or test PostgreSQL
python-backend.challenge: Write a full test suite for your Products API — cover all endpoints, auth flows, and edge cases with fixtures for test users and a test database.

python-backend.title: Docker & Deployment
python-backend.description: Ship your Python backend to production. Containerize with Docker, configure Gunicorn/Uvicorn for production serving, and automate deployment with CI/CD.
topics: Dockerfile for Python — slim base image, multi-stage build
GitHub Actions — run pytest, build Docker image, deploy
python-backend.challenge: Dockerize your Products API, write a docker-compose.yml with all services, and deploy to Fly.io via a GitHub Actions pipeline that runs tests before deploying.

python-backend.title: Microservices and Advanced Architecture
python-backend.description: Design and implement production-grade microservice systems using Python with event-driven communication and CQRS patterns. Learn how gRPC enables efficient inter-service contracts and how to decompose monoliths into bounded contexts.
topics: gRPC service definitions and protobuf schemas
Service mesh basics and sidecar proxies
API gateway patterns and BFF design
python-backend.challenge: Build a two-service order and inventory system connected via RabbitMQ events, exposing a gRPC API between services and implementing a saga for rollback on failure.

python-backend.title: Scaling, Observability and Production Operations
python-backend.description: Operate Python services at scale by implementing distributed tracing, structured logging, and SLO-driven alerting pipelines. Understand load balancing strategies, incident management, and on-call processes that keep production healthy.
topics: Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry and Jaeger
Structured logging and log aggregation pipelines
Prometheus metrics instrumentation and alerting rules
Defining and measuring SLOs and error budgets
Horizontal scaling and load balancer configuration
Chaos engineering and failure injection basics
python-backend.challenge: Instrument a FastAPI service with OpenTelemetry traces and Prometheus metrics, define two SLOs with burn-rate alerts in Grafana, and write a runbook for the most likely alert scenario.

grpc-dev.title: RPC & Protocol Buffers Foundations
grpc-dev.description: Understand remote procedure calls and the binary format that powers gRPC. Learn why Protobuf beats JSON for service-to-service traffic.
topics: What RPC is & how it differs from REST
grpc-dev.challenge: Write your first .proto file describing a simple service and compile it with protoc.

grpc-dev.title: Defining Services with Protobuf
grpc-dev.description: Design clean, evolvable APIs with Protocol Buffers. Master messages, services and the proto3 syntax.
grpc-dev.challenge: Model a complete order service schema with messages, enums and four RPC methods.

grpc-dev.title: gRPC Fundamentals
grpc-dev.description: Build your first working gRPC service and client. Understand stubs, channels and the unary call lifecycle.
grpc-dev.challenge: Implement a unary gRPC service and call it from a client and from grpcurl.

grpc-dev.title: Streaming RPCs
grpc-dev.description: Go beyond request/response. Implement server, client and bidirectional streaming over HTTP/2.
grpc-dev.challenge: Build a live metrics service using server-side streaming and a chat using bidirectional streaming.

grpc-dev.title: gRPC in Go
grpc-dev.description: Implement high-performance gRPC services in Go, the language gRPC feels most at home in.
grpc-dev.challenge: Implement your order service in Go with unary and streaming methods and a Go client.

grpc-dev.title: gRPC in Node.js
grpc-dev.description: Bring gRPC to the Node ecosystem. Generate types and build services and clients in TypeScript.
grpc-dev.challenge: Build a Node.js gRPC client in TypeScript that talks to your Go order service.

grpc-dev.title: Interceptors, Auth & Metadata
grpc-dev.description: Add cross-cutting concerns to gRPC. Use interceptors and metadata for auth, logging and tracing, secured with TLS.
grpc-dev.challenge: Add a JWT-checking interceptor and enable mutual TLS between your services.

grpc-dev.title: Error Handling & Deadlines
grpc-dev.description: Make gRPC robust. Use rich error models, propagate deadlines, and add retries for reliability.
grpc-dev.challenge: Add structured error details, deadlines and a retry policy to your order client.

grpc-dev.title: gRPC-Web, Gateways & Testing
grpc-dev.description: Bridge gRPC to browsers and REST clients, and test your services thoroughly.
grpc-dev.challenge: Expose your service as REST via grpc-gateway and load-test the gRPC endpoint with ghz.

grpc-dev.title: Performance & Deployment
grpc-dev.description: Ship and scale gRPC in production. Manage schemas, load balance, and deploy on Kubernetes.
grpc-dev.challenge: Manage your protos with Buf, containerize the services, and deploy them load-balanced on Kubernetes.

node-dev.title: JavaScript & TypeScript Foundations
node-dev.description: A Node developer lives in JavaScript and, increasingly, TypeScript. Solidify the language features the backend relies on every day.
node-dev.challenge: Build a typed CLI tool that reads JSON, transforms it, and writes results with zero 'any'.

node-dev.title: Node.js Runtime Internals
node-dev.description: Understand what makes Node tick — the event loop, the module system, and the core APIs you build everything on.
topics: CommonJS vs ES modules in Node
Core modules — fs, path, os, events
node-dev.challenge: Build a small file-watcher using core modules and the EventEmitter pattern.

node-dev.title: Async Patterns & Streams
node-dev.description: Master Node's asynchronous nature. Work with streams for efficient I/O and avoid the common async pitfalls.
node-dev.challenge: Build a streaming CSV-to-JSON converter that processes a large file without loading it into memory.

node-dev.title: Building APIs with Fastify
node-dev.description: Build fast, well-structured HTTP APIs with Fastify — a high-performance, schema-first Node framework.
node-dev.challenge: Build a REST API for an inventory service with Fastify, JSON schema validation and plugins.

node-dev.title: Data with Prisma & PostgreSQL
node-dev.description: Persist data with a typed ORM. Model relations, run migrations, and query safely with Prisma against PostgreSQL.
node-dev.challenge: Back your inventory API with PostgreSQL via Prisma, including relations and a transactional stock update.

node-dev.title: Authentication & Security
node-dev.description: Secure your Node API. Implement authentication, protect routes, and apply standard hardening practices.
node-dev.challenge: Add JWT auth with refresh tokens, role-based route guards, and rate limiting to your API.

node-dev.title: Validation, Errors & Architecture
node-dev.description: Make your codebase robust and maintainable. Validate inputs, handle errors consistently, and structure layers cleanly.
node-dev.challenge: Refactor your API into route/service/repository layers with Zod validation and a global error handler.

node-dev.title: Testing
node-dev.description: Test your Node services with confidence. Cover units, integration, and full API flows.
node-dev.challenge: Write unit tests for services and Supertest integration tests covering auth and CRUD flows.

node-dev.title: Performance, Caching & Queues
node-dev.description: Scale your Node app. Cache hot data, offload heavy work to background jobs, and tune performance.
node-dev.challenge: Add Redis caching to hot endpoints and move report generation to a BullMQ background queue.

node-dev.title: Observability & Deployment
node-dev.description: Ship and operate your Node service. Add logging, metrics and tracing, containerize, and automate deployment.
node-dev.challenge: Add structured logging and tracing, containerize the API, and deploy it via a CI pipeline.
